summary: node-fsagent exfiltrates /root/.codex via npm publish using an obfuscated hardcoded auth token
details: archive-sender.js consists of an IIFE that eval()s a base64-encoded payload. The decoded payload creates a tar archive of the /root/.codex directory, writes a synthetic package (name=node-fsagent, version=5.0.<timestamp>) with the archive embedded as data.bin, drops a.npmrc containing an XOR-decoded hardcoded npm authToken for registry.npmjs.org, and runs `npm publish --access public` to upload the archive as a public npm package. The npm token is stored as two XOR-obfuscated byte arrays (_k and _d) combined at runtime and written into the generated.npmrc. The combination of base64+eval concealment, hardcoded obfuscated credential, and exfiltration by public-package publish is a confirmed supply-chain attack against any installer or agent environment that loads this file.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
